1.note: Or the leader of the assembly

8.anchor: I amassed silver and gold for myself, and the treasure of kings and provinces. I acquired male and female singers, and a harem
8.note: The meaning of the Hebrew for this phrase is uncertain.
Or over the human spirit to retain it, / and so

9.anchor: All this I saw, as I applied my mind to everything done under the sun. There is a time when a man lords it over others to his own

10.anchor: Then too, I saw the wicked buried—those who used to come and go from the holy place and receive praise
10.note: Some Hebrew manuscripts and Septuagint (Aquila); most Hebrew manuscripts and are forgotten

18.anchor: Whoever fears God will avoid all extremes.

27.note: Or the leader of the assembly

16.anchor: Woe to the land whose king was a servant

11.anchor: He has made everything beautiful in its time. He has also set eternity in the human heart; yet
11.note: Or also placed ignorance in the human heart, so that

15.anchor: and God will call the past to account.
15.note: Or God calls back the past

19.anchor: Surely the fate of human beings is like that of the animals; the same fate awaits them both: As one dies, so dies the other. All have the same breath

5.anchor: or how the body is formed
5.note: Or know how life the spirit enters the body being formed

2.anchor: All share a common destiny—the righteous and the wicked, the good and the bad,
2.note: Septuagint (Aquila), Vulgate and Syriac; Hebrew does not have .

4.anchor: Anyone who is among the living has hope
4.note: Or What then is to be chosen? With all who live, there is hope

8.note: Or the leader of the assembly

11.anchor: The words of the wise are like goads, their collected sayings like firmly embedded nails—given by one shepherd.
